vulnerability_info.Message: Deprecated: for ECDH, use the GenerateKey methods of the crypto/ecdh package; for ECDSA, use the GenerateKey function of the crypto/ecdsa package.

vulnerability_info.Message: Deprecated: for ECDH, use the crypto/ecdh package. This function accepts an encoding equivalent to that of the NewPublicKey methods in crypto/ecdh.

Slicing_Criteria.ParentFunction: ellipticCurvePreMasterSecret (publicKey []byte, privateKey []byte, c1 crypto/elliptic.Curve, c2 crypto/elliptic.Curve) ([]byte, error)

full_implementation:
func (a Algorithm) Digest(b []byte) []byte {
	switch a {
	case None:
		return nil
	case MD5:
		hash := md5.Sum(b) // #nosec
		return hash[:]
	case SHA1:
		hash := sha1.Sum(b) // #nosec
		return hash[:]
	case SHA224:
		hash := sha256.Sum224(b)
		return hash[:]
	case SHA256:
		hash := sha256.Sum256(b)
		return hash[:]
	case SHA384:
		hash := sha512.Sum384(b)
		return hash[:]
	case SHA512:
		hash := sha512.Sum512(b)
		return hash[:]
	default:
		return nil
	}
}

vulnerability_info.Message: MD5 - RFC 9155 - Deprecating MD5 Signature Hashes in TLS 1.2 and DTLS 1.2. Use MD5 in HMAC is Acceptable But Not Recommended

	switch a {
	case None:
		return nil
	case MD5:
		hash := md5.Sum(b) // #nosec
		return hash[:]
	case SHA1:

vulnerability_info.Message: SHA-224 - NIST 800-57 : In 2031 and beyond, SHA-224 shall not be used for applying cryptographic protection.
